page: Microsoft Azure E128ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type includes 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E128ds_v7 offers high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with substantial local storage on a dedicated CPU allocation. This profile is designed for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. Suitable applications include large relational databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and data warehousing.
description: Microsoft Azure E128ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv7 family, running on the x86_64 architecture via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors and is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. For storage-heavy operations, the instance includes 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. It does not feature integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high density of memory and local storage makes this server type highly efficient for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. It is designed for large-scale databases, in-memory data processing, data warehousing, and complex enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity and dedicated compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated compute cores and substantial local storage for demanding enterprise database workloads.
